mnemonics: T.S. ERRORS: Time Series Errors Require Regression Order Structure (T.S. ERRORS)
keyPoints: Time series regression models allow error terms to be serially correlated rather than independent
Standard regression assumptions are violated when errors exhibit autocorrelation
Models must account for error structure (e.g., AR, MA, ARMA processes) to produce valid inferences
commonMistakes: Applying standard OLS regression to time series data without checking for autocorrelation in residuals
Ignoring the need to model error structure when serial correlation is present
Failing to use appropriate estimation techniques (e.g., GLS) when errors are correlated
